overall.description: Third-person gameplay footage from The Witcher 3 showing Geralt exploring a lakeside rural area. The character walks from an open meadow with a distant castle view, through a ruined stone fortification with wooden palisades, and continues along a dirt path toward a small isolated house. The scene features bright daylight, lush greenery, and medieval fantasy architecture.

scene_state.functional_zone: lakeside meadow with distant castle view

agents.ego_description: walking steadily through grassy terrain toward the ruined fortification visible ahead

entered.detail: calm blue lake stretching to the horizon with a castle visible on the far shore
tall stone tower visible across the lake against the cloudy sky
large stone ruin with crumbling walls and a wooden watchtower attached
tall wooden lookout tower with a sloped roof adjacent to the stone ruins

segments.scene_description: The segment opens on a wide lakeside meadow with Geralt walking toward a distant ruined fortification. The lake and a far castle tower dominate the background while the stone ruins and wooden watchtower grow larger as the character approaches.

scene_state.functional_zone: ruined fortification entrance with wooden gate

events.description: entering the wooden gate structure of the ruined fortification

agents.ego_description: walking through the entrance gate of the ruined fortification, passing between wooden palisade walls

notable_agents.action: villager in red clothing standing near the path inside the gate
guard standing near the wooden gate entrance watching the approach

entered.detail: large wooden gate structure with vertical logs forming the entrance to the compound

segments.scene_description: Geralt enters the ruined fortification through a wooden palisade gate. Two NPCs are visible near the entrance—a villager in red and a guard. The lake and distant castle disappear from view as the character moves into the compound, with the stone ruins now dominating the immediate foreground.

scene_state.functional_zone: path alongside ruined walls with scattered debris

events.description: exiting the main gate area and turning along the outer wall past broken debris

agents.ego_description: walking along the exterior path beside the palisade wall, passing broken carts and debris

entered.detail: destroyed wooden cart with broken wheels lying on its side in the grass
scattered wooden barrels and crates near the broken cart

segments.scene_description: Moving along the exterior of the fortification, Geralt passes a broken cart and scattered barrels beside the wooden palisade wall. The main gate structure falls behind as the path curves around the perimeter of the ruins.

scene_state.functional_zone: countryside path leading to isolated wooden house

agents.ego_description: walking along the winding dirt path toward a small wooden house in the distance

entered.detail: small rustic wooden house with a sloped roof visible at the end of the path

segments.scene_description: The final segment shows Geralt walking along an open countryside path toward a small isolated wooden house. The ruined fortification and debris are left behind as the character approaches the distant building through tall grass and scattered trees.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous camera movement from a wide lakeside meadow through a ruined fortification and into the open countryside. It begins with Geralt walking across a grassy plain toward a distant ruined fortification, with a lake and a far castle tower framing the horizon. As he enters the compound through a wooden palisade gate, the distant vistas disappear and are replaced by the immediate presence of stone ruins and NPCs stationed at the entrance. The camera then tracks along the perimeter of the fortification, passing a broken cart and scattered barrels before the path curves away from the ruins. Finally, the scene opens back up into the rural landscape as Geralt leaves the debris behind, walking through tall grass and scattered trees toward a small, isolated wooden house.
